title: Day 11: Conflict Continues as Iran Installs New Leadership, No Ceasefire in Sight
detail: The US-Israel war on Iran entered its eleventh day with no ceasefire prospects. A senior Iranian official told CNN that Israel's attacks on oil and fuel depots have pushed the conflict into a 'new phase,' with Iran threatening retaliatory strikes on energy infrastructure. An Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson stated Iran has no interest in negotiations while under attack. Iran has fired over 500 ballistic missiles and nearly 2,000 drones since February 28, with roughly 40% aimed at Israel and 60% at US regional targets.

title: US Forces Have Struck Nearly 2,000 Targets in Iran Since Feb. 28
detail: US forces have struck nearly 2,000 targets in Iran since the start of Operation Epic Fury, eliminating top Iranian officials including the former Supreme Leader. Strikes have hit nuclear facilities, military infrastructure, and critical civilian infrastructure such as oil refineries. Both the US and Israel now claim to control Iranian airspace. Airwars war monitor previously noted strikes were occurring at a pace 'far surpassing other recent conflicts.'

title: US War Cost Estimated at $890M+ Per Day; Total May Exceed $10B
detail: CNN analysts estimate the conflict is costing the United States at least $890 million per day, if not more. The Center for American Progress estimated the total at $5 billion by March 3 (Day 4); the Center for Strategic and International Studies placed the first 100-hour cost at $3.7 billion. With the conflict now in its second week, total US expenditure is projected to have surpassed $10 billion.

title: Senate Democrats Condemn US Strike on Minab Girls' School; NPR Investigation Raises Questions
detail: Top Senate Democrats condemned what they called a U.S. airstrike on Shajareh Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab on February 28, saying they were 'horrified' by reports of 165–180 killed, mostly children. An NPR investigation found the strike appeared consistent with a precision airstrike on a nearby military compound, raising questions about whether outdated targeting data caused civilian deaths. Neither the US nor Israel has accepted responsibility.

title: Iranian Internet Connectivity Collapses to ~1% of Normal Levels
detail: Iran's internet connectivity has collapsed to approximately 1% of normal levels, according to OHCHR. The communications blackout isolates civilians, preventing them from understanding or documenting events, and severely limits independent verification of casualty figures and strike damage. The UN High Commissioner for Human Rights has called this weaponization of communications restrictions a grave humanitarian concern.

title: UK Allows US Use of RAF Bases for 'Limited Defensive Purpose' Against Iranian Missiles
detail: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer confirmed the US could use UK military bases for a 'specific and limited defensive purpose' to destroy Iranian missiles at source. An Iranian drone struck the UK's RAF base in Cyprus during the conflict. The UK has not participated offensively. Starmer, alongside France and Germany, had condemned Iranian counterstrikes and called for diplomacy, while stating he does 'not believe in regime change from the skies.'
